assessment_summary.cou_name: Predict peak von Mises stress and subsidence force under 1800 N compressive load to support 510(k) submission as computational evidence

evidence_keywords: benchmark re-runs when solver version changes
patch test performed on single-element cube
FEA peak pressure within 2.3% of closed-form
no formal regression test suite maintained
convergence study may not be directly applicable
sum of reaction forces at fixed boundary = 1800.3 N
geometry simplification decisions not formally documented
retroactively documented rather than prospectively planned
regulatory affairs staff who may not have FEA expertise
peak cage stress reduced by 18% when bone deformation is permitted
graft window idealization not formally studied
PEEK datasheet value is for dry-as-molded
unquantified uncertainty that is not propagated
plastic hardening curve from internal coupon testing
DIC surface strain data from 2 specimens
subsidence load at 1 mm displacement: test mean = 2,340 N ± 180 N
ASTM F2077 loading does not fully represent in vivo loading
1800 N load may be non-conservative for obese patient population
no experimental measurement of friction coefficient for this specific surface finish
no attempt to bound in vivo loads
FEA prediction of 1 mm subsidence load: 2,510 N
Max principal surface strain: FEA 4,820 με vs. DIC mean 5,110 με
FEA overpredicts stiffness but underpredicts surface strain
no reconciliation analysis has been performed

expected_factors.factor_type: Relevance of the quantities of interest
evidence_keywords: peak stress (718 MPa) exceeds fatigue limit
executive summary states peak stresses are within acceptable limits without qualification

expected_factors.factor_type: Relevance of the validation activities to the COU
evidence_keywords: ASTM F2077 loading does not fully represent in vivo loading
model represents a single geometry size L 10° lordosis
no analysis of size variants or lordosis sensitivity
acceptance criterion of ±10% appears with no technical justification
